bullets.text: Q1 companywide CR of 86.0; Personal Lines CR 85.7; personal property CR 87.2 (6.2 points better than Q1 2024); Commercial Lines CR 87.5.
Companywide NPW grew 17% YOY; Personal Lines NPW grew 20% YOY; personal auto new applications grew 32% YOY driven by peak shopping season advertising and agent incentives.
Over 36 million total PIFs at March 31, 2025, up ~5.5 million YOY (18% YOY growth in Personal Lines); personal property PIFs up 11% driven by renters growth.

Debt-to-total capital at 19.2% at Q1 end, near lower end of historical range; portfolio returned 2.2% (fixed income 2.5%, equity -5.0% amid market turbulence); 56% of portfolio in short-term securities or treasury bonds.

Personal property benefited from 3.7 points of favorable prior-year development; tariff impacts on auto parts costs, supply chain, and loss severity are being actively modeled across multiple scenarios.

Commercial auto industry (ex-Progressive) estimated at 111.4 CR for 2024; competitors taking high single-digit rate increases; Progressive expects its rates to become more competitive as industry levels rise.

Personal auto model 8.9 in 21 states (40% of auto NPW) featuring Progressive vehicle protection (mechanical breakdown coverage); Snapshot 5.0 deployed in 41 states (78% of auto NPW) with accident detection and emergency towing.
Rate activity was minimal in Q1; management monitoring tariff impacts on auto parts, parts availability, and general inflation to determine if and when rate action is warranted.

Personal property 5.0+ models elevated in 22 states (62% of homeowners NPW) with expanded peril rating; personal property rates increased 2% in Q1 (15% on trailing 12-month basis); new homeowners growth focused in lower-risk regions.
Open for business in almost all states for personal auto; peak shopping season advertising investment drove 32% new application growth; agency channel supported with reward incentives.
